title: cleome procumbens subsp. obtusa
intro: Cleome procumbens subsp. obtusa, also known as the Procumbent Spider Flower, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral compounds. The health benefits of Cleome procumbens subsp. obtusa include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat various skin conditions and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its uses and preparations may vary depending on the region and intended application.
